fixed: 375px (medium): away-team name truncated to a lone flag — 'United States – 🇧🇪…' now wraps to two lines and reads 'United States – Belgium'
393px (low): 'United States – 🇧🇪 B…' / 'Switzerland – 🇨🇴 Col…' now fully readable
375/393px (low): cycling stage finish towns clipped ('Etappe 3 (fjell): Granolle…') now show in full ('Granollers – Les Angles')
notes: Root cause: .ev-title used single-line white-space:nowrap + text-overflow:ellipsis; on phones the 84–116px channel column starved long 'Home – Away' titles.

Fix (CSS only, scoped to @media max-width:460px): let .ev-title wrap (white-space:normal, overflow-wrap:break-word, line-height 1.3) and switch the row to align-items:start with a 1px top nudge on badge/time/channel/caret so they align to the first title line instead of floating at the vertical centre of a two-line title.
Desktop (900px) is untouched — titles still fit on one line, no regression; the fix only engages at the phone breakpoint.

Re-screenshotted at 375/393/900 and read the images: all reported names now fully readable, layout stays a calm single column, no new overflow, round labels + header + 'Hva vi følger' + footer intact, must-see (Brazil – Norway) accent still restrained.
npm test: 173 passed. No data files touched.
